title: xyris nubigena
intro: Xyris nubigena, also known as the mountain yellow-eyed grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae. It is native to the Andean region in South America, specifically found in the high-altitude areas of Peru and Bolivia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, Xyris nubigena is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be dried and used as a powder or made into a tincture for its medicinal benefits.
